recording_notes: DIRECT CAMERA — phone filming from above open FLS trainer. Same setup as V15/V16. Excellent image quality. Black suture. Two mark dots visible. ~221s clip length, task ~215s.

frame_analyses.description: First instrument tip enters field; task timing starts here.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled entry; short setup before stable driving angle.

frame_analyses.description: Needle aligned to target dots and driven through Penrose with both instruments stabilizing the drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Steady needle control; drain anchored without excessive traction.

frame_analyses.description: Suture traversed both marks; trainee pulling strand through to equalize tails.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement close to marks; tail management slow, adding idle time.

frame_analyses.description: First throw formed with visible double wrap around left instrument before opposite limb is grasped.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent with surgeon's knot; good loop control; seats without tearing.

frame_analyses.description: Single opposing throw advanced with right instrument leading, left receiving tail.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand leadership switched; setup and tightening somewhat prolonged.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw formed as single wrap, left instrument leading before seating.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Alternation back to left visible; lays down securely after extra regrasping.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments repositioned for cutting; first tail presented near knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot intact; cutting phase inefficient and prolonged.

frame_analyses.description: Final cutting maneuver close to knot with both instruments converging on remaining tail.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Acceptable cut placement; several seconds of setup before second cut.

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends cut; short tails remain on knot, loose segments free in field.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final knot stable, no avulsion, no obvious residual gap.

knot_assessments.notes: Visible double wrap around left instrument before opposite limb grasped and seated.
Single opposing throw, right-led; flattens without obvious loosening.
Returns to left leadership; final knot intact after cutting.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible gap after tightening or cutting; drain secure.

technique_summary: Accurate and atraumatic suture placement with correct knot sequence (true double first throw + alternating singles). Final knot secure, no drain avulsion, no obvious residual gap. Main inefficiencies were prolonged tail management between phases and lengthy cutting sequence.
improvement_suggestions: Reduce prolonged tail equalization and regrasping after needle passage; start first throw sooner.
Use smaller, more deliberate loops during throws 2 and 3.
Bring scissors into the field earlier and present each tail closer to the knot.
strengths: Bite placement close to both target marks; clean slit traversal.
First throw visible surgeon's knot; subsequent throws alternate leading hands.
Drain securely attached; final knot stable after both tails cut.
comparison_notes: Close total-time agreement with Claude V20 (GPT-4o 215s vs Claude 218s; Δ3.0s). FLS delta 9.0 — Claude applied 8.0 penalties (3.0 placement + 5.0 long tails), GPT-4o applied 2.0 (placement only). The long-tail deduction is the key disagreement: Claude flagged ~3cm trailing tails as a significant cosmetic/quality issue, GPT-4o observed 'short tails remain on knot' at the completion frame and did not deduct. Placement and closure both agree on 'good.' Drain-closure agreement (no gap, no avulsion).
